title: How does Spring manage circular dependencies in Dependency Injection, and how would you resolve them?

answers.junior: Spring can handle circular dependencies for some beans, but it’s best to avoid them. Use setter injection if needed.
answers.middle: Spring resolves circular dependencies for singleton beans using setter or field injection. Constructor injection fails for circular dependencies. Refactor design to avoid cycles.
answers.senior: Spring exposes early references for singleton beans to resolve circular dependencies. Setter/field injection works, constructor injection fails. Refactor by extracting shared logic into a third bean.
answers.tricky: Circular dependencies are resolved via early references and proxies. Constructor injection causes BeanCurrentlyInCreationException. Refactor design or use setter injection. Document and test for maintainability.
